summary: Re-tested CorteQS Diaspora Globe after RLS fix. Backend: 13/13 pytest pass — RLS bug confirmed fixed (login → token → /auth/me → /pins → POST /pins → /pins/mine → admin 403 guard → logout all green). Frontend: globe + 25 pins + 5-category filter rail with correct counts, signup → user-chip, login/logout, search, filter all work. Two real frontend bugs discovered around the Pin-ekle (add-pin) flow.

ui_bugs.issue: The platform badge in the bottom-right corner OVERLAYS the [data-testid='add-pin-fab'] button and intercepts pointer events. Without Playwright's force=True, the click goes to the badge. Real users cannot toggle picking mode. Fix: increase z-index of the FAB container (currently z-20) above the badge, or shift the FAB to bottom-left / center-right so it does not overlap with the injected platform badge.

Even with force=True clicks (which bypass the overlay and dispatch directly on the FAB DOM node), the FAB text remains 'Pin ekle' instead of changing to 'Haritada konum seç…' and globe clicks do NOT open AddPinModal. addMode toggle appears not to take effect. Could be due to (a) the click event still leaking to a parent / canvas handler that resets state, (b) some race with auth refresh, or (c) StrictMode + impure effect. Repro: signup → wait for user-chip → click [data-testid='add-pin-fab'] (force=True) → inner_text remains 'Pin ekle', class still has 'bg-[#ffd166]' (yellow, not pink). Subsequent canvas mouse.click() at multiple positions (center, ocean) never opens [data-testid='add-pin-modal']. Needs main-agent debug.

integration_issues.flow: Add Pin end-to-end (FAB → globe click → AddPinModal → submit → toast)
integration_issues.issue: Blocked by the two FAB bugs above. Could not reach AddPinModal via UI. Backend POST /api/pins works fine via curl/pytest, so it is purely a UI wiring issue.

Typing 'berlin' in [data-testid='search-input'] and waiting 900ms sometimes shows search-results=False, but at 1500ms+ the dropdown is visible and shows 'Berlin 52.52, 13.41 · hızlı'. Not a bug per se, but document the debounce (~600ms) + render delay so tests use ≥1500ms wait.

issues: FAB and Emergent badge stacked in the same corner — visually crowded and functionally broken (overlay).

action_items: FIX: Raise z-index of the Pin-ekle FAB above the Emergent badge OR move it to a non-conflicting corner (e.g., bottom-left or bottom-center). Right now real users cannot click it.

DEBUG: Why setAddMode toggle has no effect even when click reaches the FAB. Add a console.log inside the FAB onClick to confirm it fires; check if AuthContext refresh() or any other effect is racing and resetting addMode. Verify in production build (StrictMode is dev-only) that the issue persists.
Optionally: increase debounce wait or expose a loading indicator inside [data-testid='search-results'] for nicer e2e waits.
Admin flow not E2E-tested (deferred per request); verified via curl in previous iteration. To E2E: append test email to ADMIN_EMAILS, restart backend, login again, navigate to /admin.
critical_code_review_comments: App.js: FAB container <div className='absolute bottom-6 right-6 z-20'> conflicts with Emergent badge in same corner. Either change to bottom-left or bump z-index >50.
App.js onGlobeClick(): only acts when addMode=true. If addMode never flips true (current bug), pin creation is unreachable via UI.

server.py: separated sb_auth (anon) vs sb (service_role) clients — fix verified end-to-end through pytest. ensure_profile is read-then-insert, idempotent. login() except is now narrower. RCA from iteration_1 fully resolved.
Frontend logs 401 on initial /api/auth/me when no token exists — expected, but consider adding `validateStatus` so the console isn't spammed with red errors on cold visits.

success_rate.frontend: 70% (globe load, pin count, signup, login, logout, search, filter all OK; add-pin FAB picking flow blocked)

test_credentials: Created during this run: test_<timestamp>@example.com / test123456 — login verified working. Existing user testuser_1778362787@example.com / test123456 from /app/memory/test_credentials.md remains valid.
seed_data_creation: No new seed; existing 25 sample pins still present and visible.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend fully green via /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py (just run `pytest backend/tests/backend_test.py -v`). On frontend, verify add-pin flow once main agent fixes (a) FAB overlay (z-index / position vs Emergent badge) and (b) addMode state actually toggling on click. Repro: signup, user-chip appears, click [data-testid='add-pin-fab'] — text MUST change to 'Haritada konum seç…' and class must include 'bg-[#ff6b9d]' (pink). Then click ocean area on canvas → [data-testid='add-pin-modal'] should open with lat/lng filled. Submit with name/city/hood → toast 'Pin gönderildi!' and modal closes. Search debounce: use ≥1500ms wait after fill before checking [data-testid='search-results'].
rca_of_the_issue: Bug 1 (FAB overlay): Playwright confirmed the injected platform badge sat on top of the FAB at z-stack level. The FAB's parent has z-20 but the badge was injected at a higher layer by the platform. Mitigation: move FAB container or set className to `z-[60]` and add `pointer-events-auto`. Bug 2 (addMode not toggling): even when click reaches the FAB element directly via force=True, the rendered text stays 'Pin ekle' and the class stays yellow (`bg-[#ffd166]`), implying setAddMode(m=>!m) is being called but immediately reset by another effect, OR the click handler is not actually firing. Recommend adding a `console.log('FAB clicked', addMode)` inside the onClick to confirm, and auditing useEffect deps in MainScreen for any side effect that calls setAddMode(false).
